Class Player

java.lang.Object
model.Player
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class Player extends Object implements Serializable
A class representing one of the two players in a given fight. Holds references to the player's full team as well as the current active Mon.
Author:
Isa Abdulrazaq
See Also:

  • Field Details

    • serialVersionUID

      private static final long serialVersionUID
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • mons

      private Mon[] mons
      The Player's full team. Max size 6.

    • pokedex

      private Mon[] pokedex

    • activeMon

      private Mon activeMon

  • Constructor Details

    • Player

      public Player(Mon[] monArray) throws IllegalArgumentException
      Constructs a new Player object with the team of Mon's passed as a param
      Parameters:
      monArray - a team of Mon's (max 6, min 1) associated with the player
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the team size is incorrect.

  • Method Details

    • addToDex

      public void addToDex(Mon monToAdd)
      Adds a Mon to Dex list, only if Mon is not already in Dex
      Parameters:
      Mon - monToAdd

    • isInDex

      public boolean isInDex(Mon monToCheck)
      Checks if a Mon is in Dex list
      Parameters:
      Mon - monToCheck
      Returns:
      true if Mon is in Dex, false otherwise

    • getTeam

      public Mon[] getTeam()
      Getter for team
      Returns:
      Mon[] teamList

    • teamSize

      public int teamSize()
      Getter for size of Team
      Returns:
      int mons.length

    • pokedexSize

      public int pokedexSize()
      Getter Dexlist size
      Returns:
      int size of Dex

    • replaceMon

      public void replaceMon(int i, Mon mon)
      Replaces mon of team at index i with Mon mon.
      Parameters:
      int - i for index, Mon mon for new Mon

    • monIsInTeam

      public boolean monIsInTeam(Mon mon)
      Check to see if Mon mon is in Team
      Parameters:
      Mon - mon
      Returns:
      true if Mon mon is in Team, false otherwise

    • getPokedexAt

      public Mon getPokedexAt(int i)
      Getter for mon in Dex at int index
      Parameters:
      int - i for index
      Returns:
      Mon mon in pokedex[i]

    • getMonAt

      public Mon getMonAt(int i)
      Getter for mon in Team at int index
      Parameters:
      int - i for index
      Returns:
      Mon mon in team[i]

    • resetMons

      public void resetMons()
      Resets all mons by healing them and resetting PP on Moves

    • healAllMons

      public void healAllMons()
      Heals all mons on Player's Team

    • getActiveMon

      public Mon getActiveMon()
      Returns a reference to the current Mon in combat
      Returns:
      activeMon the current Mon in combat

    • getMon

      public Mon getMon(int index)
      Returns a Mon at the given index. Used for the swap menu.
      Parameters:
      index - Index of the Mon to return in the players team
      Returns:
      Mon at the index

    • switchMon

      public void switchMon(int index) throws IllegalArgumentException
      Changes the activeMon to the one at the given index in mons.
      Parameters:
      index - the index of Mon in mons to be switched to active combat.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the Mon pointed at by index has fainted or mons.length < index.

    • swapMon

      public void swapMon(int i, int j)
      Swaps Mons on team, swaps index i and j
      Parameters:
      int - i, int j

    • livingMonCount

      public int livingMonCount()
      Counts the number of Mons still able to be switched to/battle with.
      Returns:
      count the number of Mons that have not fainted.

    • hasLost

      public boolean hasLost()
      Determines if the player has lost the battle Players lose when they no longer have any living Mons
      Returns:
      true/false depending on whether livingMonCount() == 0.
